personal_info.name: Hasan
personal_info.title: Founder & Managing Director of H Studio | Event Planner | Event Media Director | Hospitality & Protocol Specialist
personal_info.bio:
I am the founder of H studio; a company specialized in the design, planning and execution of large events, protocol management, content production, live broadcast and media operations.
My professional path began in my teenage years and from the heart of the media and broadcast space. After receiving specialized degrees in photography and videography in 2014, I entered the professional job market and from the very beginning I was active in advertising projects, branding and video content production.
While studying hotel management and protocol and earning the title of top student, my practical experience in the field of protocol, hospitality and event management developed. This combination led me to be at the intersection of media, hotel management, protocol and event management.
Continuing on the path, by managing and executing more than 500 projects and collaborating with more than 50 national and international brands and organizations, H Studio was formed as a company specialized in the fields of Event Operations, Media Production and Protocol Management.

education.university: University of Applied Science and Technology
University of Applied Science and Technology

roles.title: Founder & Managing Director
description: H Studio is a specialized event media and production company with a team size of 6 – 20 professionals, based in Kish Island.
The company focuses on: Conferences, Seminars, Exhibitions, Sports Events, Corporate Events, Live Broadcast Productions, Hospitality & Protocol Services.

roles.title: Protocol Manager
description: Managed VIP protocol operations, guest experience coordination, ceremonial planning, and executive hospitality services.

roles.title: Protocol Manager
description: Managed protocol and hospitality arrangements for official gatherings and industry events.

projects.name: ECO Tourism Symposium
projects.role: Project Director | Event Operations Director | Media Director
projects.description: Directed and managed the Tourism Symposium of ECO Member Countries, coordinating media, operations, protocol, and event execution.
projects.client: Gerdoun Tourism Group – Kish Free Zone Organization
projects.detail:
### Project Overview

The ECO Tourism Symposium 2025 was an international tourism event held on Kish Island with the participation of member countries of the Economic Cooperation Organization (ECO). The event aimed to strengthen regional tourism cooperation and promote cross-border collaboration within the ECO member states.

This high-level event brought together official delegates, industry leaders, and key stakeholders from multiple countries in a highly sensitive and strategically important setting.

---

### Challenges

This project was considered a high-sensitivity international event due to:

- Presence of high-ranking official delegates from multiple countries
- Significant reputational importance for the host country
- Zero-margin-for-error execution environment
- Simultaneous coordination of security, protocol, media, and operations
- High-pressure real-time decision making during execution

---

### Responsibilities

- Full operational design and execution management of the event
- Leadership of a 10-member multidisciplinary team
- Coordination between protocol, security, and production units
- On-site event flow supervision and real-time control
- Media operations management (news coverage, content production, distribution)
- Quality control of all visual and media outputs prior to release

---

### Execution Team

A 10-person specialized team including:

- Content production unit
- Video production & filming crew
- Real-time editing team (rapid post-production)
- News and media distribution team
- On-ground coordination and event operations staff

---

### Key Achievements

- Successful execution of a high-level international tourism symposium
- Full real-time media coverage with simultaneous production and publishing
- Seamless coordination of multiple operational layers under pressure
- Continuous content delivery during the event lifecycle
- Zero critical operational or security failures during execution

---

### Key Highlight

Due to the presence of high-level international delegates and official representatives, the project required extremely strict operational control and risk management.

In parallel, the media team was responsible for:

- Real-time news production
- Rapid content editing and validation
- Quality assurance before publication
- Controlled and strategic media distribution

This significantly increased the complexity and operational intensity of the project.
